browser_playback: pass: finite duration, positive dimensions, no media error

scenes.storyboard_visual_object: An aquifer transect carrying a continuous sinusoidal boundary wave across three observation wells.
scenes.entry_content_summary: A boundary sinusoid enters the aquifer transect while the first observation well establishes distance.
scenes.midpoint_content_summary: The moving crest reaches successive wells as retained wave copies become smaller and later.
scenes.settled_content_summary: Three observation-well responses show a continuous inland wave with attenuated envelopes and accumulated phase.

scenes.storyboard_visual_object: The retained wave bifurcates into amplitude and phase diffusivity gauges.
scenes.entry_content_summary: The periodic groundwater wave remains visible before either classical diffusivity estimate is introduced.
scenes.midpoint_content_summary: Amplitude attenuation and phase accumulation branch from the same measured wave into separate gauges.
scenes.settled_content_summary: The amplitude and phase diffusivity needles separate, exposing inconsistent interpretations of one signal.

scenes.storyboard_visual_object: A stack of past gradient arrows weighted by a fading causal kernel.
scenes.entry_content_summary: Past hydraulic-gradient arrows appear behind the present state in a time-ordered history stack.
scenes.midpoint_content_summary: A fading causal kernel assigns progressively smaller weights to older hydraulic-gradient contributions.
scenes.settled_content_summary: Weighted gradient history assembles present flux, showing memory rather than a rigid signal translation.

scenes.storyboard_visual_object: Past gradients sliding under a memory kernel and summing into q.
scenes.entry_content_summary: Ordered hydraulic-gradient traces begin moving beneath the visible causal memory envelope.
scenes.midpoint_content_summary: Kernel weights scale the past-gradient contributions as they converge toward the present time.
scenes.settled_content_summary: The weighted history becomes present Darcy flux and the kernel area retains effective conductivity.

scenes.storyboard_visual_object: The memory envelope folds into tau_q and tau_h clocks on opposite sides of Darcy's law.
scenes.entry_content_summary: A two-channel memory envelope appears between separate flux and head response clocks.
scenes.midpoint_content_summary: The memory representation folds into tau_q and tau_h on opposite sides of Darcy response.
scenes.settled_content_summary: The local constitutive relation displays distinct derivatives governed by the two relaxation times.

scenes.storyboard_visual_object: Flux entering a control volume while stored head changes inside it.
scenes.entry_content_summary: Boundary flux arrows enter and leave a groundwater control volume containing stored hydraulic head.
scenes.midpoint_content_summary: The imbalance between incoming and outgoing flux changes the stored head inside the volume.
scenes.settled_content_summary: Mass conservation combines with the two-lag constitutive law to produce the head equation.

scenes.storyboard_visual_object: A rotating boundary phasor extrudes into a decaying wave with envelope alpha and crest gradient beta.
scenes.entry_content_summary: A rotating boundary phasor initiates a periodic head wave at the aquifer edge.
scenes.midpoint_content_summary: The inland wave loses amplitude while its crest accumulates phase across observation distance.
scenes.settled_content_summary: The complex wavenumber separates into attenuation alpha and phase gradient beta in the harmonic solution.

scenes.storyboard_visual_object: Alpha and beta gauges transform into a two-dimensional log eta regime map centered on eta equal to one.
scenes.entry_content_summary: The mismatch surface is organized by dimensionless frequency and the head-to-flux lag ratio.
scenes.midpoint_content_summary: The eta field separates attenuation-derived and phase-derived diffusivities across omega_D and theta.
scenes.settled_content_summary: A vertical theta=1 boundary marks eta=1, the classical amplitude-phase agreement condition.

scenes.storyboard_visual_object: Boundary and well hydrographs rotate into spectra whose complex ratio yields magnitude and angle.
scenes.entry_content_summary: Boundary-stage and observation-well hydrographs establish the two measured periodic time series.
scenes.midpoint_content_summary: The records rotate into frequency-domain spectra with a visible complex ratio between them.
scenes.settled_content_summary: The transfer function separates into amplitude magnitude and phase argument for joint interpretation.

scenes.storyboard_visual_object: Tuolumne and Meghna amplitude-phase points attract one analytical curve per site.
scenes.entry_content_summary: Tuolumne and Meghna panels introduce amplitude and phase observations digitized from the published figures.
scenes.midpoint_content_summary: Published fitted median curves develop against the same observed amplitude and phase data.
scenes.settled_content_summary: The source-figure reconstructions show one reported parameter set alongside both signatures at each field site.

scenes.storyboard_visual_object: Classical and lagging residual stacks balance against their parameter counts on an AIC scale.
scenes.entry_content_summary: Classical and lagging residual contributions enter an information-criterion comparison for each field site.
scenes.midpoint_content_summary: Improved fit competes visibly with the penalty for the two additional lag parameters.
scenes.settled_content_summary: The displayed AIC values favor the lagging representation at both analyzed aquifers.

scenes.storyboard_visual_object: Each amplitude-phase point expands into a Gaussian error ellipse sampled 5000 times.
scenes.entry_content_summary: Observed amplitude-phase values appear with Gaussian error regions derived from measurement uncertainty.
scenes.midpoint_content_summary: Random draws begin populating each error region while preserving the observed frequency structure.
scenes.settled_content_summary: Five thousand measurement-consistent amplitude-phase realizations form the uncertainty input ensemble.

scenes.storyboard_visual_object: Representative sampled data sets pull one parameter marker through bounded D tau_q tau_h space.
scenes.entry_content_summary: One sampled amplitude-phase data set enters bounded D, tau_q, and tau_h parameter space.
scenes.midpoint_content_summary: The bounded fitting operation moves a parameter marker toward the realization-specific optimum.
scenes.settled_content_summary: Repeated refitting leaves one bounded parameter estimate for every sampled measurement realization.

scenes.storyboard_visual_object: An interquartile fence closes around the parameter cloud and fades out outlying refits.
scenes.entry_content_summary: The refitted parameter cloud includes a dense central population and several distant estimates.
scenes.midpoint_content_summary: Interquartile fences close around the central cloud and identify aberrant parameter refits.
scenes.settled_content_summary: Outlying refits fade while the accepted interquartile-filtered parameter ensemble remains visible.

scenes.storyboard_visual_object: Accepted parameter clouds collapse into mean markers with one-standard-deviation bands.
scenes.entry_content_summary: Accepted D, tau_q, and tau_h estimates occupy their respective parameter intervals.
scenes.midpoint_content_summary: Mean markers and one-standard-deviation widths emerge from each filtered parameter distribution.
scenes.settled_content_summary: The completed interval summary reports uncertainty as both central estimate and distribution width.

scenes.storyboard_visual_object: Analytical and finite-difference amplitude-phase curves trace over the same aquifer transect.
scenes.entry_content_summary: Separate panels introduce analytical and finite-difference predictions for amplitude and phase.
scenes.midpoint_content_summary: Numerical markers advance across both attenuation and phase curves against the analytical solutions.
scenes.settled_content_summary: Amplitude and phase predictions coincide across distance, validating both components of the analytical response.

scenes.storyboard_visual_object: Morris sensitivity curves for amplitude and phase change rank with scaled distance.
scenes.entry_content_summary: Separate amplitude and phase sensitivity panels begin with lag, frequency, and distance factors.
scenes.midpoint_content_summary: Morris sensitivity curves change magnitude and ordering as scaled observation distance increases.
scenes.settled_content_summary: The settled rankings show where periodic observations are informative for each model factor.

scenes.storyboard_visual_object: The original two field waves reappear with analytical amplitude envelopes phase shifts parameter summaries and AIC comparisons.
scenes.entry_content_summary: The closing return rebuilds the Tuolumne and Meghna aquifer transects with periodic boundary forcing.
scenes.midpoint_content_summary: Wells and propagating waves expose contrasting field attenuation and phase behavior at the two sites.
scenes.settled_content_summary: The field transects resolve into reported lag parameters and AIC evidence, linking the opening puzzle to field interpretation.
